perturbation.original_text: Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information".
perturbation.changed_text: Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information"' has been removed. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because while the initial list defines what constitutes 'Highly Confidential Information,' removing the 'catch-all' phrase leaves the definition incomplete. Without it, Fox can't unilaterally designate new information as 'Highly Confidential,' contradicting the implied intent of the original clause to allow for flexibility in defining such information. The current definition creates an in-text contradiction, as it implies there is an option to designate information as Highly Confidential Information but doesn't allow it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes. Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed Fox to designate other information as 'Highly Confidential Information' at its discretion. The changed text removes this ability, creating a direct contradiction with the introductory statement in the contradicted text, which states that 'certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information")'. By removing Fox's ability to designate information as highly confidential, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction by not allowing certain information to be classified as Highly Confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees' has been removed. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement initially outlines that only company employees can access 'Highly Confidential Information', but now employees, consultants and vendors have the same status regarding this kind of information, since there is no longer a restraint about the employees. This removal suggests an undefined scope of disclosure creating uncertainty and the potential for broader dissemination, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees', but the changed text removes this limitation. The contradicted text states that 'Highly Confidential Information shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information' and reiterates that the company 'shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.' By removing the restriction limiting disclosure to employees, the changed text creates a contradiction because it implies that Highly Confidential Information could potentially be disclosed to individuals beyond employees, which directly conflicts with the contradicted text's restriction on disclosure to consultants, vendors, or agents. This creates uncertainty about who can access Highly Confidential Information, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of Company, (i) neither Fox nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Company will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Company concerning the Confidential Information, and (ii) none of Fox's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Company who is aware of the Confidential Information of Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of Company, (i) neither Fox nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Company will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Company concerning the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase '(ii) none of Fox's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Company who is aware of the Confidential Information of Company.' has been removed. This clause previously restricted Fox from soliciting Company's employees. Removing it means Fox can now solicit Company's employees which is contradictory to the start of the phrase 'Without the prior written consent of Company' since Company does not need to consent with the solicitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the prior written consent of Company, (i) neither Fox nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Company will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Company concerning the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited Fox from soliciting Company's employees for two years without Company's consent. The changed text removes this prohibition. The contradicted text states that without the prior written consent of Company, Fox cannot communicate with the employees of the company concerning confidential information. Although the contradicted text and changed text are the same, the removal of the second clause in the original text creates a situation where Fox can solicit employees, but it's unclear if that requires consent, leading to a contradiction and uncertainty around the conditionality of the initial 'Without the prior written consent of Company'.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of Fox, (i) neither Company nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Fox will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Fox concerning the Confidential Information, and (ii) none of Company's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Fox who is aware of the Confidential Information of Fox.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of Fox, (i) neither Company nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Fox will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Fox concerning the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase '(ii) none of Company's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Fox who is aware of the Confidential Information of Fox.' has been removed. This clause previously restricted Company from soliciting Fox's employees. Removing it means Company can now solicit Fox's employees which is contradictory to the start of the phrase 'Without the prior written consent of Fox' since Fox does not need to consent with the solicitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the prior written consent of Fox, (i) neither Company nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Fox will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Fox concerning the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the company from soliciting Fox's employees for two years without prior written consent. The changed text removes this prohibition, meaning the company can now solicit Fox's employees without consent. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text states that the company cannot initiate communications with employees of Fox without consent. The act of solicitation falls under the definition of 'initiate communications', so the change contradicts the initial statement that Fox's consent is needed.

perturbation.original_text: Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party,' was removed. This omission creates an in-text contradiction within section 7. Previously, the Receiving Party only had to return confidential information if a specific list was provided. Without this provision, the Receiving Party is always obligated to return the information, contradicting the original intent where returning information was conditional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the condition 'Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party'. The contradicted text still contains this condition. This creates a direct conflict because the receiving party is now unconditionally obligated to return the information, whereas previously they were only obligated to return it upon receiving a specific list. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and specific performance.' was removed. The text states that money damages are insufficient, implying the necessity of specific performance to enforce the agreement. The current text mentions other remedies, implying those remedies can substitute the impossibility of money damages for any breach of contract. This constitutes an in-text contradiction by undermining the claim that only equitable relief is suitable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages are insufficient and includes 'specific performance' as an equitable remedy. The changed text omits 'specific performance'. Because the contradicted text and the changed text are in the same location, removing 'specific performance' contradicts the statement that money damages are insufficient. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it undermines the original assertion that only equitable relief, including specific performance, is suitable, making the contract inconsistent.
